title: Enumerate regression risks when writing to live game memory
prompt:
QA review: a teammate wants to deploy a live memory-writing pin thread to a production Ken-plays-Halo session. Write a markdown doc '# Regression risks — live memory writes' with:

1. Five ways a bad write could soft-crash Halo: (a) writing to a guard page, (b) write-then-read race with the game's game-logic thread, (c) value clamped by game → visible 'bounce' in HUD, (d) writing to the wrong struct entirely (you found a shield counter that looks like health), (e) writing outside the aligned 4-byte boundary.
2. Three symptoms that mean 'abort the write loop': process handle invalidated, more than 3 consecutive WriteProcessMemory failures, PID changed mid-run.
3. Three symptoms that mean 'safe to continue': reads confirm persistence for 10 frames, HUD shows expected value, player hasn't died.
4. A watchdog helper outline: tick 500ms, check (a)(b)(c), if any fail → signal stop_event.
5. Rollback: how do you undo a pin if you realize you pinned the wrong value? (revert to pre-pin snapshot per address).

Dense bullets. No prose intro.
